response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings over 16 years of broad software development experience, open-source engagement, and some evidence of knowledge-sharing and mentoring. However, he falls short on several hard requirements for this role: his German is self-described as 'Basic,' far below the mandatory C1 threshold for an IT consulting position that requires advising German-speaking clients; there is no Python in his skill set despite it being a desired technical skill; and his resume shows no explicit UML modeling, formal IT architecture design, or risk/compliance work. Most critically, evidence of leading and managing a team at the level expected for a Head of Development role is thin — he has functioned as a senior individual contributor and founder of a solo open-source project, but has not demonstrably held people management or executive technical leadership responsibilities.
strengths: 16+ years of diverse, polyglot software development experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
Active open-source contributor and founder (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial), demonstrating initiative and community engagement
Consistent track record of knowledge sharing through workshops, conference talks, and community events, indicating communication and mentoring ability
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' — well below the C1 minimum required for a client-facing consulting leadership role in a German-speaking market
No Python in the skill set and no mention of UML or formal architecture modeling, both of which are explicitly listed as desired or required
No demonstrated experience leading or managing a development team, handling client-facing project delivery accountability, or working with IT security, risk management, or compliance frameworks
